parametersPage.confirmSafetyBody: affects the robot's movement or safety. The new value applies immediately to the running robot.
parametersPage.arrayReadOnlyTooltip: Arrays are read-only: safely editing a vector requires a per-element interface, not yet available here.
parametersPage.loadError: Unable to load parameters — is the robot connected?
parametersPage.liveWarningTitle: These settings apply live and are not saved to the YAML
parametersPage.liveWarningDescription: Changes take effect immediately on the running robot and are lost at the next ROS2 restart. For persistent values, use the Settings page.
parametersPage.tierGuardDescription: These low-level parameters drive pose estimation, motor gains, safety thresholds and more. A bad value can make the robot move unexpectedly or degrade localisation. The controls stay locked until you confirm.

mowerStatus.restartMowgliConfirm: This will restart the MowgliNext container.
mowerStatus.restartBoardConfirm: Resets the STM32 firmware (NVIC_SystemReset). Use it to unstick a frozen board — e.g. the IMU returning NaN. The motors/blade stop during the reset (~1 s).
mowerStatus.restartPiConfirm: The system will restart. You will lose the connection temporarily.
mowerStatus.shutdownPiConfirm: The system will shut down. Physical access will be required to power it back on.
mowerStatus.rearmEmergencyTooltip: Re-arm the emergency stop (the firmware decides whether the latch releases)

mowerActions.sendHomeTitle: Send the robot back to base?
mowerActions.sendHomeBody: The robot will plan a route to the base and drive itself there.
mowerActions.sendHomeHint: Make sure the area between the robot and the base is clear and that the base position is correctly set.

flashBoard.advancedAlertMessage: These settings control battery charging and the physical safety limits
flashBoard.advancedAlertDescription: Incorrect voltage or current values can damage the battery or the board. Only change these fields if you know exactly what you are doing.
flashBoard.disableEmergencyWarning: ⚠ Disables the firmware emergency stop (wheels lifted, tilt, stop button). Use only on a test bench, never on a robot with a blade fitted.
flashBoard.confirmVerifyParams: Please verify the following parameters before flashing:
flashBoard.confirmWrongValuesWarning: Wrong voltage or current values can damage your battery or hardware!
flashBoard.firmwareDirectoryTooltip: Path to the firmware directory inside the cloned repository (containing stm32/ros_usbnode)
flashBoard.maxMpsTooltip: Max speed in meters per second
flashBoard.tickPerMTooltip: Number of wheel ticks per meter
flashBoard.limitVoltage150MATooltip: Voltage limit during slow charge in Volts
flashBoard.batChargeCutoffVoltageTooltip: Max battery voltage allowed in Volts
flashBoard.oneWheelLiftEmergencyMillisTooltip: Time in ms before emergency when one wheel is lifted
flashBoard.bothWheelsLiftEmergencyMillisTooltip: Time in ms before emergency when both wheels are lifted
flashBoard.tiltEmergencyMillisTooltip: Time in ms before emergency when mower is tilted
flashBoard.stopButtonEmergencyMillisTooltip: Time in ms before emergency when stop button is pressed
flashBoard.playButtonClearEmergencyMillisTooltip: Time in ms to hold play button to clear emergency

dockingSection.dockingBehaviourDescription: Set how the robot approaches and leaves the base. The base position (X, Y, Yaw) is defined from the map editor.
dockingSection.undockDistanceTooltip: Reverse distance when leaving the base
dockingSection.approachDistanceTooltip: Staging-point distance in front of the base
dockingSection.chargerDetectionTooltip: Use the charging voltage to confirm docking
dockingSection.maxRetriesTooltip: Number of docking attempts before giving up
dockingSection.chargingThresholdTooltip: Battery current (A) above which SimpleChargingDock considers the base reached. Higher = the robot pushes further into the contacts before stopping; lower = stops earlier. ~0.3 A gives stable contact on the YardForce charger.
dockingSection.approachOvershootTooltip: Distance (m) travelled beyond the base pose to seat the charging contacts firmly. ~0.05 m compensates for residual RTK noise / lever-arm offset.
dockingSection.baseHeadingUncertaintyTooltip: Standard deviation (rad) of the base heading used to seed the fusion graph at charging contact. Lower = the calibrated heading is trusted more.

diagnosticsPage.subtitleContainerStopped: A container is stopped — check the system
diagnosticsPage.subtitleOutOfRange: GPS, battery or temperature out of range
diagnosticsPage.subtitleWatch: Working — a few points to watch
diagnosticsPage.advancedGnssUnavailable: Advanced GNSS fields are not available for this backend.
diagnosticsPage.fusionGraphDescPart1: GTSAM iSAM2 factor graph. Save persists nodes + scans to
diagnosticsPage.fusionGraphDescPart2: Clear wipes the graph and waits for the next GPS fix or set_pose to re-initialize.
diagnosticsPage.calibrationStartedDesc: Running 3 forward/back cycles plus optional dock pre-phase. This may take up to 2 minutes.
diagnosticsPage.magCalibrationStartedDesc: Robot will run a figure-8 (~30 s). Make sure there's at least 1.5 m clear in front and behind.
diagnosticsPage.dockPoseNotSet: Dock pose not yet set in mowgli_robot.yaml. Run the calibration while the robot is docked, or place the dock manually from the map view.
diagnosticsPage.noImuCalibration: No imu_calibration.txt yet — hardware_bridge will auto-calibrate on the next dock.
diagnosticsPage.magFusionOffPart2: on calibrate_imu_yaw_node to include the rotation phase.
diagnosticsPage.perWheelTempNote: Per-wheel motor temperatures + currents are not exposed by the firmware; only the blade motor reports those (see Hardware Status below).

mowgliNextPage.sublineCharging: On the dock, {{current}} A coming in.
mowgliNextPage.sublineEmergency: Secure the area, then restart with the green button.
mowgliNextPage.sublineIdle: Tap Play to start a mow, or let the schedule take over.
mowgliNextPage.emergencyConfirmBody: This triggers the hardware emergency stop (blades + movement). Re-arm manually afterwards. Continue?

onboardingPage.expertFieldsNotWired: These expert fields are saved, but the backend API that translates them into receiver commands is not wired up yet. For the UM982, "UM982 recommended" will map to CONFIG SIGNALGROUP 3 6. In the meantime, the vendor-neutral signal profile above is enough.
onboardingPage.gpsRestartedWaitRtk: GPS restarted — wait for RTK Fix
onboardingPage.welcomeIntro: A few short steps and you're ready to mow. Nothing here is set in stone -- you can always change these settings later from the Settings page.
onboardingPage.welcomeCardRobotDesc: Select your mower model and firmware
onboardingPage.welcomeCardGpsDesc: Configure your position and RTK corrections
onboardingPage.welcomeCardSensorsDesc: Visually position LiDAR, IMU, and GPS on the robot
onboardingPage.robotModelIntro: Select your mower model. This pre-fills hardware parameters like wheel size, battery voltage, and blade dimensions.
onboardingPage.customConfigDesc: You can fine-tune all hardware parameters in Settings after completing onboarding.
onboardingPage.ntripIntro: RTK corrections give centimetre accuracy. Pick a correction network and the nearest base station on the map — credentials for the free networks are filled in for you. You'll configure the GPS receiver next.
onboardingPage.gpsIntro: Configure the GNSS receiver with vendor-neutral profiles first, then enable Expert mode only when you need receiver-family-specific overrides. The map origin (datum) is set later, once the receiver has had a moment to acquire an RTK fix.
onboardingPage.gpsRestartingAlertTitle: GNSS receiver is restarting to apply your serial and NTRIP settings
onboardingPage.gpsRestartingAlertDesc: Wait ~10–30 s for RTK Fix to come back before setting the datum. Receiver profile apply, factory reset, and GNSS restart actions are available from the receiver actions panel below.
onboardingPage.liveGnssStatus: Live GNSS status: {{status}}. The normal onboarding UI stays vendor-neutral; family-specific tuning lives in Expert mode.
onboardingPage.normalVsExpert: Normal settings are vendor-neutral. Expert settings are receiver-family specific.
onboardingPage.signalProfileTooltip: High-level constellation and signal preset. This is all most users need to touch.
onboardingPage.baudTooltip: Serial speed between the robot and the GPS receiver. Saved to the receiver flash so both sides always match — you only set it once.
onboardingPage.savedToFlashTitle: Saved to the GPS receiver flash
onboardingPage.savedToFlashDesc: Baud and signal profile are firmware-side settings: on save they are written to the GPS receiver's flash and the receiver briefly reconnects. 460800 is the safe baud for USB serial links; 921600 needs a robust UART or adapter.
onboardingPage.expertGnssSettingsDesc: Receiver-family selection, raw serial wiring, and vendor-specific overrides live here.
onboardingPage.receiverProfileTooltip: Low-level receiver command set. Backend translation to receiver-specific commands is still being wired — leave on the default unless you know you need it.
onboardingPage.saveContinueReceiverTitle: Save & Continue to start the receiver
onboardingPage.saveContinueReceiverDesc: Saving this step writes your signal profile and baud to the GPS receiver and restarts it automatically (NTRIP corrections were set in the previous step). Acquiring an RTK fix can take 30 s to a few minutes — that happens in the background while you set the datum next.
onboardingPage.datumSetFailedTitle: Failed to set datum from GPS
onboardingPage.datumSetFailedDesc: Could not capture the current GPS position as the map datum.
onboardingPage.datumIntro: Anchor the robot's local map to a GPS coordinate near your docking station. This becomes the (0, 0) origin of every later map and mowing area. Drop it on the dock if possible.
onboardingPage.datumCoordinatesHint: Either enter the coordinate manually (right-click your dock in Google Maps to copy lat/lon), or capture it directly from the robot once it has an RTK Fix.
onboardingPage.useCurrentGpsPositionWaiting: Use current GPS position (waiting for RTK Fix)
onboardingPage.gpsContainerRestartingTitle: GPS container is restarting to apply your NTRIP / serial settings
onboardingPage.gpsContainerRestartingDesc: Wait ~10–30 s for RTK Fix to come back before setting the datum.
onboardingPage.rtkFloatWarning: RTK Float gives ~10–20 cm accuracy and drifts when corrections lapse. Wait for RTK Fix (σ ~3 mm) before anchoring the datum.
onboardingPage.noRtkFixWarning: Without RTK Fix the datum can be metres off, which silently breaks every later mow. Make sure NTRIP corrections are flowing and the antenna has clear sky.
onboardingPage.sensorPlacementIntro: Position your sensors on the robot. Drag them on the top-down view or use the precise numeric inputs. These positions tell the robot exactly where each sensor is mounted relative to the chassis centre.
onboardingPage.calibrationIntro: The robot drives itself through a short routine to learn how its sensors are mounted. Without this step the robot drifts in odom, can dock at an angle, and loses its heading when GPS corrections lapse. Plan for up to 2 minutes of autonomous motion — stand clear, collision_monitor stays armed.
onboardingPage.measureDockPoseBody: (when started on the dock): the robot reverses ~2 m under RTK GPS to capture the dock's lat/lon and heading, then writes them to
onboardingPage.measureImuYawBody: : 3 forward/backward cycles at 0.5 m/s let the accelerometer's body-frame impulse be compared to the wheel-derived acceleration so the IMU's rotation around base_link is recovered.
onboardingPage.measurePitchRollBody: : the stationary baseline windows expose any non-level mounting (1° offsets here matter — gyro integration drifts otherwise).
onboardingPage.measureMagBody: (if a mag is publishing): a slow figure-8 fits the ellipsoid offsets so tilt-compensated yaw is usable as an absolute heading source.
onboardingPage.preflightDockSuffix: if you want the dock pose recorded too — otherwise just leave it parked with ≥ 1.5 m of clear space ahead and behind.
onboardingPage.preflightNtrip: NTRIP corrections flowing and an RTK Fix nearby (otherwise the dock pre-phase is skipped, but IMU calibration still runs).
onboardingPage.preflightEmergency: No active emergency, blade off, lid closed.
onboardingPage.preflightDontTouch: Don't move or touch the robot during the run — every bump shows up as accelerometer noise and widens the std-dev.
onboardingPage.calibRunningTitle: Calibration running — robot is driving itself
onboardingPage.calibRunningDesc: Forward leg, pause, backward leg. Stand clear. Motion stops automatically. May take up to 2 minutes (longer when the dock pre-phase runs).
onboardingPage.dockPoseUpdated: Dock pose updated: yaw={{yaw}}° (σ {{sigma}}°, displacement {{displacement}} m).
onboardingPage.calibFailedHint: Hint: drive faster or longer so the accelerometer sees a clear forward and backward impulse along the body X axis.
onboardingPage.skipStepDesc: If you have already calibrated this robot from the Diagnostics page (or know your imu_yaw value), use the Next button. The Complete step will warn you if calibration is still missing.
onboardingPage.firmwareIntro: If this is a new build, you need to flash the Mowgli firmware onto your motherboard. If your firmware is already up to date, you can skip this step.
onboardingPage.flashWarningTitle: Flashing will rewrite your motherboard firmware
onboardingPage.flashWarningDesc: Make sure your mower is connected via USB and powered on. Wrong voltage settings can damage hardware.
onboardingPage.applyingConfigSubtitle: Restarting the mower service with your new settings. This takes a few seconds.
onboardingPage.allSetSubtitle: Your mower is configured and ready to go. Head to the Map to draw your first mowing area, or check the Dashboard to monitor your robot.
onboardingPage.calibPendingSuffix_one: is not calibrated yet. Without these the robot will drift in odom and may dock at an angle.
onboardingPage.calibPendingSuffix_other: are not calibrated yet. Without these the robot will drift in odom and may dock at an angle.
onboardingPage.restartFailedTitle: Could not restart the mower service
onboardingPage.restartFailedDesc: You may need to restart it manually.
onboardingPage.persistGnssError: Failed to save GNSS settings before running the receiver action

schedulePage.deleteScheduleConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this schedule?
schedulePage.pickAStarterDescTap: One tap to create a schedule. Tune the days, time and area afterwards.
schedulePage.pickAStarterDescClick: One click to create a schedule. Tune the days, time and area afterwards.
schedulePage.noSchedulesYet: No schedules yet. Pick a starter below or create a custom run.

statisticsPage.firstSessionWillAppearHere: Your first session will appear here.
statisticsPage.clearAllDescription: This permanently deletes every recorded session. This cannot be undone.

dashboardHome.heroSubline: Moving at 0.42 m/s · wet grass detected, speed reduced by 12 %.

conceptStatistics.title: Statistics
conceptStatistics.headlineRest: in a row. A fine season.
conceptStatistics.heatmapSubtitle: 186 active days · 184.3 km mowed in total

containerRestart.timeoutDescription: ROS2 did not restart within {{seconds}} s.

settingsManager.gpsRestartSuccess: GPS restarted — wait ~10–30 s for RTK Fix

CRITICAL_BATTERY_NAV_FAILED.label: Return failed
CRITICAL_BATTERY_NAV_FAILED.friendly: Critical battery and unable to return

RAIN_WAITING.label: Waiting for dry
RAIN_WAITING.friendly: Waiting for the rain to stop

RAIN_TIMEOUT.label: Rain timeout
RAIN_TIMEOUT.friendly: Gave up waiting for dry weather

mapEditorToolbar.clickToPlaceDock: Click on map to place dock

mapImportOpenMower.title: Import OpenMower map
mapImportOpenMower.replaceWarningMessage: Apply replaces the current MowgliNext map
mapImportOpenMower.replaceWarningDescription: Clicking Apply will clear every existing area and dock pose, then write the areas shown below. Use Backup Map from the More menu first if you want a rollback file.
mapImportOpenMower.noDockingStation: No active docking station in source map — dock pose will not be touched.
mapImportOpenMower.datumShiftDescription: OpenMower coordinates were translated to land in the MowgliNext map frame. If this looks wrong, double-check both datums.
mapImportOpenMower.noMowingAreasMessage: No mowing areas in this file
mapImportOpenMower.noMowingAreasDescription: MowgliNext requires at least one mow area. Apply is disabled — close this modal and pick a different map.json.

mapTrackedObstacles.outsideAreaMessage: Obstacle is outside every mowing area
mapTrackedObstacles.outsideAreaDescription: Draw a mowing area that covers this obstacle before promoting it.
mapTrackedObstacles.confirmBodyPrefix: This adds the polygon as a permanent keepout in
mapTrackedObstacles.confirmBodySuffix: The robot will plan around it from now on (until you remove it).

mapEditing.unableToMatchAreaForObstacle: Unable to match an area for this obstacle
mapEditing.splitTargetNotValidPolygon: Split target is not a valid polygon
mapEditing.lineMustCrossTwice: Line must cross the area at least twice to split it
mapEditing.noParentAreaForObstacleSplit: No parent area found for obstacle split
mapEditing.unableToCombineAreas: Unable to combine areas. Do they overlap?
mapEditing.couldNotTraceShapeFromEmoji: Could not trace a shape from this emoji
mapEditing.bothSelectionsMustBePolygons: Both selections must be polygon areas
mapEditing.subtractFailed: Subtract failed — areas may not overlap, or result is not a simple polygon
mapEditing.selectionMustBePolygon: Selection must be a polygon area
mapEditing.drawLineAcrossArea: Draw a line across the area to split it

mapFiles.bagImportComingSoonDescription: Convert your map.bag to map.json on the source robot first (OpenMower 1.x auto-converts at boot), then re-import the .json. See docs/IMPORT_OPENMOWER_MAP.md §6.
mapFiles.openMowerMapImportedDescription: Areas + dock pose written. /map should refresh shortly.

mapEditHistory.discardContent: You have unsaved map changes. If you cancel now, all changes will be lost.

uncurated.description: Uncurated parameter — exposed in the Expert profile only.

tool_width.label: Tool width
tool_width.description: Effective blade cut width. Drives both the mowed-area stamp radius and the F2C swath spacing.

transit_speed.label: Transit speed
transit_speed.description: Travel speed when driving between areas or to the dock.

mowing_speed.label: Mowing speed
mowing_speed.description: Forward speed while mowing a swath.

num_headland_passes.label: Headland passes
num_headland_passes.description: Number of concentric border rings mowed before the back-and-forth swaths.

headland_width.label: Headland width
headland_width.description: Total border band width; rings are spaced by the tool width.

swath_overlap.label: Swath overlap
swath_overlap.description: Extra overlap between adjacent swaths to avoid thin un-mowed strips.

swath_angle.label: Swath angle
swath_angle.description: Fixed mowing-line angle. Leave at auto unless you need a specific stripe direction.

chassis_safety_inset.label: Chassis safety inset
chassis_safety_inset.description: Pre-inset of the field boundary to keep the chassis inside the polygon.

progress_timeout_sec.label: Progress timeout
progress_timeout_sec.description: How long the controller may make no progress before recovery kicks in.

dock_approach_overshoot.label: Dock approach overshoot
dock_approach_overshoot.description: Extra distance driven past the dock pose to guarantee charge-contact.

use_gps_dock_detection.label: GPS dock detection
use_gps_dock_detection.description: Detect the dock from the GPS dock pose instead of relying on isDocked().

dock_pose_yaw_sigma_rad.label: Dock yaw uncertainty
dock_pose_yaw_sigma_rad.description: Assumed heading uncertainty of the configured dock pose.

declination_deg.label: Magnetic declination
declination_deg.description: Local magnetic declination applied to the magnetometer yaw.

mag_yaw_variance.label: Mag yaw variance
mag_yaw_variance.description: Sensor-noise variance for the magnetometer yaw factor.

enable_mag_cal.label: Collect mag calibration
enable_mag_cal.description: Gather magnetometer samples while RTK-Fixed to estimate hard/soft-iron offsets.

min_horizontal_uT.label: Min horizontal field
min_horizontal_uT.description: Minimum horizontal field magnitude below which the magnetometer yaw is rejected.

datum_lat.label: Datum latitude
datum_lat.description: Map origin latitude. Set during onboarding from an RTK-Fixed position.

datum_lon.label: Datum longitude
datum_lon.description: Map origin longitude. Set during onboarding from an RTK-Fixed position.

node_period_s.label: Graph node period
node_period_s.description: Cadence at which the factor graph adds a new Pose2 node.

use_scan_matching.label: LiDAR scan matching
use_scan_matching.description: Add LiDAR scan-match between-factors to ride through RTK-Float windows.

use_loop_closure.label: LiDAR loop closure
use_loop_closure.description: Add loop-closure factors from revisited LiDAR scans.

icp_max_iter.label: ICP max iterations
icp_max_iter.description: Maximum iterations for the scan-match ICP solve.

icp_max_corresp_dist.label: ICP correspondence dist
icp_max_corresp_dist.description: Maximum point-pair distance considered a correspondence.

icp_max_rmse_m.label: ICP max RMSE
icp_max_rmse_m.description: Reject a scan match whose RMSE exceeds this.

dock_blank_range.label: Dock blank range
dock_blank_range.description: Blank LiDAR returns within this range of the dock to avoid self-detection.

post_undock_blank_sec.label: Post-undock blank
post_undock_blank_sec.description: Suppress obstacle detection for this long after undocking.

min_obstacle_z_m.label: Min obstacle height
min_obstacle_z_m.description: Ignore LiDAR points below this height (ground filter).

max_obstacle_z_m.label: Max obstacle height
max_obstacle_z_m.description: Ignore LiDAR points above this height.

lidar_height_m.label: LiDAR mount height
lidar_height_m.description: Height of the LiDAR above ground, used by the ground filter.

angular_rate_kp.label: Angular rate Kp
angular_rate_kp.description: Proportional gain of the ROS-side angular-rate assist loop.

angular_rate_ki.label: Angular rate Ki
angular_rate_ki.description: Integral gain of the angular-rate assist loop.

angular_rate_kff.label: Angular rate Kff
angular_rate_kff.description: Feed-forward gain of the angular-rate assist loop.

imu_cal_samples.label: IMU cal samples
imu_cal_samples.description: Number of samples averaged during the on-dock IMU bias calibration.

imu_cal_auto_rest_sec.label: IMU rest window
imu_cal_auto_rest_sec.description: How long the robot must be detected stationary before a bias calibration starts.

imu_cal_periodic_recal_sec.label: IMU recal interval
imu_cal_periodic_recal_sec.description: How often the IMU bias is re-estimated while parked.

battery_warn_pct.label: Battery warn %
battery_warn_pct.description: Battery level below which a warning diagnostic is raised.

battery_error_pct.label: Battery error %
battery_error_pct.description: Battery level below which an error diagnostic is raised.

motor_temp_warn_c.label: Motor temp warn
motor_temp_warn_c.description: Motor temperature above which a warning is raised.

motor_temp_error_c.label: Motor temp error
motor_temp_error_c.description: Motor temperature above which an error is raised.

balanced.label: Balanced
balanced.description: Recommended default profile. Uses a balanced mix of available GNSS constellations and signal bands.

high_precision.label: Maximum Accuracy
high_precision.description: Prioritizes multi-band and high-precision signals for the best positioning performance.

all_signals.label: Maximum Compatibility
all_signals.description: Uses the widest set of commonly supported signals for maximum receiver and regional compatibility.

minimal.label: Low Bandwidth
minimal.description: Reduces signal and observation load when bandwidth, processing power or correction transport capacity is limited.

custom.label: Custom
custom.description: Reserved for future user-provided profile files or raw receiver command sets when backend support is available.

gnssConfig.signalProfileHelpText: Signal profiles are vendor-neutral. Different receiver families can map the same profile to different signal and constellation choices.
gnssConfig.signalProfileCustomHelpText: Custom will later support user-provided profile files or raw receiver command sets from Expert/Developer mode. Upload and raw-command backend support are not wired yet.

unicore.title: Vendor-Specific Settings
unicore.description: Use these only when the generic GNSS abstractions are not enough. UM982 signal-group tuning stays here instead of leaking into the main user workflow.

signalGroup.label: Signal Group Preset
signalGroup.tooltip: Choose a Unicore signal-group preset, then fine-tune the raw value only if needed.
signalGroup.helpText: Normal settings are vendor-neutral. Expert settings are receiver-family specific. For UM982, "UM982 recommended" maps to CONFIG SIGNALGROUP 3 6.

pvtAlgorithm.label: PVT Algorithm
pvtAlgorithm.tooltip: Optional Unicore-specific PVT algorithm override that the future backend translator can map to vendor commands.
pvtAlgorithm.helpText: Leave blank to keep the receiver default/current value. This field is saved now, but backend apply translation is still TODO.

rtkReliability.label: RTK Reliability
rtkReliability.tooltip: Optional Unicore-specific RTK reliability tuning passed through only in Expert mode.

YardForce500.label: YardForce Classic 500
YardForce500.description: Most common model. 28V battery, 18cm blade, rear-wheel drive.

YardForce500B.label: YardForce 500B
YardForce500B.description: 500 B variant with different blade motor UART and panel layout.

YardForceSA650.label: YardForce SA650
YardForceSA650.description: Larger model (570x390mm) with higher encoder resolution.

YardForce900ECO.label: YardForce 900 ECO
YardForce900ECO.description: Same chassis as SA650 (570x390mm), larger battery.

CUSTOM.label: Custom Robot
CUSTOM.description: Manually configure all hardware parameters for a custom build.

settingsHardware.robotModelDescription: Select your mower to auto-fill hardware dimensions.
settingsHardware.encoderTicksPerMeterTooltip: Encoder ticks per metre of wheel travel
settingsHardware.chassisCenterXTooltip: Distance from base_link to chassis centre

settingsDriveMotor.savedLiveDescription: Saving stores these in mowgli_robot.yaml and pushes them to the drive controller immediately — no ROS2 restart needed. They persist across reboots: on every boot the robot re-sends the saved values to the firmware. (The firmware itself has no storage, so it runs its built-in defaults only for the brief moment before the controller reconnects, then re-validates and clamps the values you saved.)
settingsDriveMotor.wheelVelocityPidDescription: Closed-loop gains the STM32 firmware uses to track each wheel's commanded speed. Higher Kp/Ki give stiffer tracking; too high causes oscillation or overshoot. Kd is normally 0.
settingsDriveMotor.kiTooltip: Integral gain (PWM per m/s·s) — bridges the static-friction deadband
settingsDriveMotor.integralLimitTooltip: Anti-windup clamp on the integral term (PWM, motor max 255)
settingsDriveMotor.pwmPerMpsTooltip: Open-loop velocity→PWM feedforward scale. Dominant drive term; also sets the idle/deadband mapping. Change with care.

common.undockDistanceTooltip: Used only when undock is enabled.

applied.title: Drive tuning applied
applied.description: The recommended drive parameters were persisted into mowgli_robot.yaml and synced in Settings.

loadReportFailed.title: Failed to load the last drive tuning report

ffStarted.title: Odometry / feed-forward calibration started
ffStarted.applyDescription: The calibration starts from the current live hardware_bridge values on pass 1, runs through /cmd_vel_tuning, then applies the recommendation live only if the run completes successfully.
ffStarted.reportOnlyDescription: This recommendation-only run starts from the current live hardware_bridge values on pass 1, uses /cmd_vel_tuning -> twist_mux, and will not persist any parameter.

ffStartFailed.title: Failed to start odometry / feed-forward calibration

pidStarted.title: PID auto-tune started
pidStarted.applyDescription: The autotune starts from the current live hardware_bridge values on pass 1, runs through /cmd_vel_tuning, and will persist the final recommendation only after a successful run.
pidStarted.reportOnlyDescription: This run is recommendation-only, starts from the current live hardware_bridge values on pass 1, and restores the original live parameters afterwards.

emergency.title: Emergency active
emergency.description: Calibration assistants are blocked while emergency is active or latched.

onDock.title: Robot appears to be on the dock
onDock.description: The assistants will refuse to move unless you explicitly enable the undock option in the launch dialog.

confirm.title: Rollback the last drive tuning?
confirm.bodyPrefix: This restores the last backup generated by
confirm.bodySuffix: , and writes the restored values back to
confirm.warning: Use this only when you want to undo the last accepted tuning result.

motionProfile.title: Conservative motion profile
motionProfile.description: The assistants use a dedicated tuning lane: stamped Twist commands on /cmd_vel_tuning, routed through twist_mux to /cmd_vel, with soft ramps and cmd_vel = 0 between motion segments. Pass 1 starts from the live hardware_bridge values unless an explicit reset-to-profile mode is enabled later.

onDock.description: Enable undock explicitly below if you want the assistant to reverse out before starting.

workflow.title: Workflow
workflow.description: The robot runs straight passes through /cmd_vel, compares wheel odometry against RTK/GPS when available, refines ticks_per_meter and wheel_pid_pwm_per_mps, then writes recommendations to a YAML report. Apply is optional.

fields.odomTimeoutTooltip: Lost-odometry safety only applies while the robot is still commanded above the low-speed threshold.

resultApply.title: Successful run will persist the recommended feed-forward values
resultApply.description: ticks_per_meter and wheel_pid_pwm_per_mps will be saved into mowgli_robot.yaml and kept live in hardware_bridge.

resultReportOnly.description: The assistant will generate a YAML report, use the current live hardware_bridge values for pass 1, and restore the original live drive parameters afterwards.

precheck.title: Feed-forward / odometry not validated yet
precheck.description: PID auto-tune should run only after ticks_per_meter and wheel_pid_pwm_per_mps were validated. You can still continue, but the recommendation quality may be poor.

onDock.description: Enable undock explicitly below if you want the PID auto-tune to leave the dock before running.

workflow.title: Teleop-based step response
workflow.description: This autotune follows the dedicated /cmd_vel_tuning -> twist_mux path, starting from the live hardware_bridge values on pass 1 and using conservative ramps: 0 → 0.2, 0.2 → 0.3, 0.3 → 0.1, 0.1 → 0. The backend monitors overshoot, settling, oscillation, integral saturation, and wheel imbalance.

resultApply.title: Successful run will persist the recommended PID gains
resultApply.description: wheel_pid_kp, wheel_pid_ki, wheel_pid_kd, and wheel_pid_integral_limit will be saved into mowgli_robot.yaml and kept live in hardware_bridge.

resultReportOnly.description: The assistant will start from the live hardware_bridge values on pass 1, then restore the original live wheel PID gains after the report is generated.

report.title: Last drive tuning report

failure.title: Last run ended with a movement fault

report.empty: No drive tuning report available yet.

trials.title: Trial summary ({{count}})
trials.line2: overshoot {{overshoot}} | settling {{settling}} | stall {{stall}} | osc {{osc}} | live osc {{liveOsc}} | quality {{quality}}

backend.statusFetchFailed: Failed to fetch drive tuning status

rollback.successMessage: Drive tuning rollback applied live and persisted to mowgli_robot.yaml

errors.jobAlreadyRunning: a drive tuning job is already running
errors.backupDidNotContainParameters: backup file did not contain drive parameters
errors.persistenceAfterApply: drive tuning applied live, but mowgli_robot.yaml persistence failed
errors.reportReadAfterRun: drive tuning finished, but the report could not be read: {{error}}
errors.listReports: failed to list drive tuning reports: {{details}}

feedForward.stall: A feed-forward pass stalled or produced no wheel motion. Re-run after checking mechanics, traction power, and odometry.
feedForward.liveOscillation: Live oscillation was observed during feed-forward calibration. Review mechanics and feed-forward before accepting the result.
feedForward.noAcceptedRtk: Feed-forward report exists, but no RTK/GPS-backed odometry validation was accepted.
feedForward.validatedExcellent: Validated with excellent odometry ({{odomPct}}%) and acceptable feed-forward speed error ({{speedPct}}%).
feedForward.validatedAcceptable: Validated with acceptable odometry ({{odomPct}}%) and feed-forward speed error ({{speedPct}}%).
feedForward.postOscillationWarning: Feed-forward calibration completed with a post-analysis oscillation warning (odom {{odomPct}}%, speed {{speedPct}}%). Review the report before accepting the result.
feedForward.completedWithWarnings: Calibration completed with warnings (odom {{odomPct}}%, speed {{speedPct}}%). Review trial notes before accepting the result.

pid.stopBehaviorReviewBraking: Stop behavior warning: residual motion detected after zero-speed command. Stop trials were excluded from PID gain selection, but braking/control should be reviewed before accepting this tune.
pid.stopBehaviorExcluded: Stop behavior warning: residual motion detected after zero-speed command. Stop trials were excluded from PID gain selection.
pid.noTrials: No PID step-response report recorded yet.
pid.stall: PID report shows a stall or no-motion step. Re-run after checking mechanics and feed-forward.
pid.liveOscillation: Live oscillation was observed during PID tuning. Re-run after reducing aggression or reviewing feed-forward.
pid.overshootReached: PID overshoot reached {{overshootPct}}% on at least one step.
pid.integralSaturation: PID report suggests sustained integral saturation under load. Review KI and integral limit before accepting the result.
pid.postOscillationUsable: Post-analysis oscillation detected but no live oscillation was observed; max overshoot {{overshootPct}}% remains usable for conservative tuning.
pid.completedWithWarnings: PID response completed with warnings; max overshoot {{overshootPct}}%. Review trial notes before accepting the result.
pid.zeroSpeedExcludedSuffix: Zero-speed stop trials were excluded from gain selection.
pid.validated: Validated PID step response with max overshoot {{overshootPct}}%.

settingsBattery.batteryThresholdsDescription: Set voltage and percentage levels that control when to dock and when to resume mowing.
settingsBattery.criticalRecoveryTooltip: Leave the critical-battery state once recharged above this %. Hysteresis upper bound, must be greater than Critical.

settingsSafety.alertMessage: Safety settings protect the robot and its surroundings
settingsSafety.alertDescription: Firmware is the sole safety authority. These settings configure firmware behavior — disabling them reduces protection.
settingsSafety.motorTemperatureLimitsDescription: Blade motor stops above the high threshold and resumes below the low threshold (hysteresis).
settingsSafety.stopAboveTooltip: Stop blade motor above this temperature
settingsSafety.resumeBelowTooltip: Resume blade motor below this temperature
settingsSafety.maxDetourDistanceTooltip: Maximum distance to detour around an obstacle before giving up

settingsMowing.mowingMotorDescription: Disable for testing without the blade spinning.
settingsMowing.transitSpeedTooltip: Speed when going to/from dock or between zones
settingsMowing.headlandWidthTooltip: Width of perimeter strip mowed before the inner field (F2C ConstHL inset). Wired to coverage_server.default_headland_width.
settingsMowing.headlandPassesTooltip: Number of concentric perimeter rings before the inner field. 0 = auto (ceil(headland_width / tool_width)). >0 forces exactly that count regardless of headland_width. Wired to coverage_server.num_headland_passes.
settingsMowing.chassisSafetyInsetTooltip: Pre-shrink the operator polygon by this many metres before F2C plans. Defaults to chassis_width / 2 so the chassis edge stays inside the polygon under perfect tracking. Wired to coverage_server.chassis_safety_inset.
settingsMowing.minTurningRadiusTooltip: F2C Robot::setMinTurningRadius — bounds the CC-Dubins arc radius between swaths. Coverage runs CONTINUOUS arcs, so this must be wide enough for MPPI to TRACK the turnaround: too small (~0.05 m) is a near-cusp the controller hitches on; ~0.12 m is a gentle Ω-turn that fits the headland band. Wired to coverage_server.min_turning_radius.
settingsMowing.mowAngleTooltip: Mowing angle offset in degrees (-1 = auto)
settingsMowing.angleIncrementTooltip: Rotate mowing angle each full pass
settingsMowing.outlinePassesTooltip: Number of perimeter passes before fill

settingsNavigation.goalTolerancesDescription: How close the robot needs to get to a target position before considering it reached. Tighter tolerances mean more precision but may cause oscillation.
settingsNavigation.progressTimeoutTooltip: Seconds without forward progress before recovery kicks in

settingsSensors.lidarDescriptionPart1: Enable if your robot has a LiDAR. Also flips
settingsSensors.lidarDescriptionPart2: so the factor graph fuses LiDAR scans. Fine-tune those in the Localization tab.
settingsSensors.imuBiasCalibrationDescription: The hardware bridge re-estimates IMU gyro/accel bias automatically while the robot is parked and stationary on the dock. These tune how it samples.
settingsSensors.calibrationSamplesTooltip: Number of stationary IMU samples averaged per bias calibration.
settingsSensors.restWindowBeforeCalTooltip: How long the robot must be detected stationary before a calibration starts.
settingsSensors.periodicRecalIntervalTooltip: Re-estimate the IMU bias every N seconds while docked. 0 disables periodic recal.

settingsRain.rainBehaviorDescription: What the robot should do when it detects rain.
settingsRain.rainModeDockDescription: Return to dock when rain detected
settingsRain.rainModeDockUntilDryDescription: Dock and wait for rain to stop + delay
settingsRain.resumeDelayTooltip: Minutes to wait after rain stops before resuming
settingsRain.debounceTooltip: Seconds of continuous rain signal before triggering

settingsAdvanced.advancedParametersDescription: These are raw YAML parameters not covered by the sections above. Edit with care — invalid values can break the robot configuration.

settingsPositioning.datumSetFromGps: Datum set from current GPS position
settingsPositioning.mapOriginDescription: The GPS coordinate used as origin for the local map. Place it near your docking station.
settingsPositioning.useCurrentGpsPosition: Use current GPS position (requires RTK fix)
settingsPositioning.liveGnssStatus: Live Universal GNSS status: {{status}}. The main UI stays vendor-neutral; expert mode exposes receiver-family-specific tuning only when needed.
settingsPositioning.gnssProfilesDescription: Normal settings are vendor-neutral. Expert settings are receiver-family specific and stay hidden until you explicitly enable them.
settingsPositioning.signalProfileTooltip: High-level constellation and signal preset. This is all most users need to touch.
settingsPositioning.baudTooltip: Serial speed between the robot and the GPS receiver. Saved to the receiver flash so both sides always match — you only set it once.
settingsPositioning.savedToFlashTitle: Saved to the GPS receiver flash
settingsPositioning.savedToFlashIntro: Baud and signal profile are firmware-side settings: on save they are written to the GPS receiver's flash and the receiver briefly reconnects.
settingsPositioning.savedToFlashSafeBaud: is the safe baud for USB serial links;
settingsPositioning.savedToFlashFastBaud: needs a robust UART or adapter.
settingsPositioning.expertGnssDescription: Receiver-family selection, raw serial wiring, and vendor-specific overrides live here. Keep these at their defaults unless you know the receiver-side implications.
settingsPositioning.receiverProfileTooltip: Low-level receiver command set. Backend translation to receiver-specific commands is still being wired — leave on the default unless you know you need it.
settingsPositioning.expertScopeDescription: Signal-group presets, raw signal-group values, PVT algorithm, RTK reliability, RTK timeout, and DGPS timeout are stored here for future backend translation. Raw command textarea, dry-run plan, and reset/apply tooling still need a dedicated API.

settingsLocalization.scanMatchingSummary: Add ICP between-factors from /scan to the graph.
settingsLocalization.scanMatchingDetail: Each new graph node runs ICP against the previous scan and adds a BetweenFactor with the relative motion. Fights GPS dropouts but costs ~5 ms/tick at 10 Hz.
settingsLocalization.loopClosureSummary: Search past scans for revisits and add LC factors.
settingsLocalization.loopClosureDetail: Triggers a candidate search around each new node (5 m radius, 10 min minimum age). Successful loop closures pull drift back to the originally-mapped pose, even mid-session.
settingsLocalization.magYawSummary: Fuse tilt-compensated mag yaw as a yaw unary factor.
settingsLocalization.magYawDetail: Off by default — motor-induced bias makes the magnetometer unreliable on most chassis. Enable only after running mag calibration with motors-off and validating a stable |B|.
settingsLocalization.mapFrameLocalizerMid: is the sole map-frame fuser — wheel + IMU + GPS + COG yaw, plus the optional LiDAR factors below. It also publishes the local
settingsLocalization.mapFrameLocalizerTfPrefix: TF, so there is no separate
settingsLocalization.lidarObstacleIntro: Always on (when the LiDAR driver is running). The Nav2 costmap obstacle layer and
settingsLocalization.lidarObstacleOutro: directly to stop the robot before it hits something — this is independent of the factor-graph LiDAR options below.
settingsLocalization.lidarTogglePrefix: Toggle the LiDAR driver itself in
settingsLocalization.lidarLocalizationOutro: as scan-matching between-factors and loop-closure factors so the map-frame pose can ride through multi-minute RTK-Float windows.
settingsLocalization.magCalibrationIntroSuffix: is enabled above. Calibration collects hard/soft-iron samples while RTK-Fixed; the rest tune when the mag yaw is trusted and how strongly.
settingsLocalization.collectCalibrationDescription: Gather magnetometer samples while RTK-Fixed to estimate hard/soft-iron offsets. Run with motors off, then disable.
settingsLocalization.magneticDeclinationTooltip: Local magnetic declination applied to the magnetometer yaw (France ≈ 1.5°).
settingsLocalization.minHorizontalFieldTooltip: Minimum horizontal field magnitude for a valid yaw. Below this the mag yaw is rejected.
settingsLocalization.magYawVarianceTooltip: Sensor-noise variance (rad²) for the magnetometer yaw unary factor. Higher = trusted less.

settingsGnssReceiver.summaryPartialFailure: GNSS action finished with partial failure
settingsGnssReceiver.summaryPartialFailureDesc: Review warnings, command output, and restart status below.
settingsGnssReceiver.summaryFailedDesc: Review command output and warnings below.
settingsGnssReceiver.summarySuccessDesc: Command output and backend warnings are shown below.
settingsGnssReceiver.notifyPartialFailureDesc: Review the backend warnings and restart result in the GNSS action panel.
settingsGnssReceiver.notifyFailedDesc: Review the GNSS action panel for stdout/stderr and warnings.
settingsGnssReceiver.confirmApplyBody: The GPS sidecar will be stopped, the receiver profile will be applied in a one-shot GNSS container, and the GPS sidecar will be restarted only if the apply succeeds.
settingsGnssReceiver.confirmApplyWarning: Do not disconnect power or USB/serial wiring while the receiver profile is being applied.
settingsGnssReceiver.confirmFactoryResetTitle: Factory reset receiver and re-apply profile?
settingsGnssReceiver.confirmFactoryResetDanger: Factory reset clears receiver settings before rebuilding the selected profile.
settingsGnssReceiver.confirmFactoryResetBody: The backend will stop the GPS sidecar, run the persistent factory-reset flow, then re-apply the selected saved profile and restart GPS only if that flow succeeds.
settingsGnssReceiver.confirmFactoryResetWarning: Use this only when you intentionally want to rebuild receiver configuration from a clean state.
settingsGnssReceiver.actionInProgressDesc: Waiting for the GNSS backend API to return command output, warnings, and restart status.

settingsNtrip.title: NTRIP RTK Corrections
settingsNtrip.disabledWarning: Without NTRIP, GPS accuracy is ~2–5 m — not enough for autonomous mowing.
settingsNtrip.intro: Pick a correction network, then choose the nearest base station on the map. Credentials for the free networks are filled in for you.
settingsNtrip.loadProviderError: Could not load stations from {{provider}}. Check the caster is reachable.
settingsNtrip.loadFreeNetworksError: Could not load the free networks.
settingsNtrip.baseStationsCount: {{count}} base stations — click the nearest one

robotComponentEditor.dockPoseSetFromGpsDescription: Captured the dock position from the averaged GPS fix and persisted to mowgli_robot.yaml. The map indicator jumps to the new pose on the next /map publish.
robotComponentEditor.setDockFromPoseTitle: Set dock pose from current robot position?
robotComponentEditor.setDockConfirmIntro2: right now and writes them to
robotComponentEditor.setDockConfirmIntro3: as the new dock pose. The IMU auto-calibration that runs on the next docking will refine the yaw if it drifts.
robotComponentEditor.setDockConfirmFooter: Make sure the robot is sitting on the dock and facing the direction it should approach from. The new pose persists across container restarts.
robotComponentEditor.dragSensorsHint: Drag sensors on the top-down robot view to set their X/Y position. Use the rotation handle (small circle) to set sensor yaw. The grid spacing is 5 cm. Robot dimensions are read from the /robot_description topic.
robotComponentEditor.autoCalibrateImuTooltip: Auto-calibrate IMU mounting yaw (robot drives itself ~0.6 m forward then back)
robotComponentEditor.compassBearingHint: Compass bearing: 0° = N, 90° = E, 180° = S, 270° = W. Hold a phone compass behind the dock pointing the way the robot enters and read the bearing off it. Auto-captured on first charge by dock_yaw_to_set_pose
robotComponentEditor.captureIntro1: Park the robot on the dock facing the approach direction, then click below. Captures
robotComponentEditor.captureIntro2: at once — this is the most reliable way to seed the dock pose for a fresh map.
robotComponentEditor.robotNow: Robot now: x={{x}} m, y={{y}} m, bearing={{bearing}}°
robotComponentEditor.tooltipNotCharging: Place robot on dock with charging contacts engaged before setting the reference pose
robotComponentEditor.tooltipCapturePose: Capture the robot's current pose as the canonical dock reference
robotComponentEditor.coordinatesHint: Coordinates are relative to base_link (centre of rear wheel axis). X+ = forward, Y+ = left, Z+ = up. Yaw is rotation around Z axis in degrees (displayed) / radians (stored).
robotComponentEditor.imuCalibIntro2: and then back to the start, taking roughly
robotComponentEditor.imuCalibBullet2b: of clear space in front and behind.
robotComponentEditor.imuCalibBullet3: Any active emergency must be cleared first.
robotComponentEditor.imuCalibCollisionMonitor: Collision monitor stays armed — obstacles will stop the motion.
robotComponentEditor.imuCalibTechnical: Estimates the mounting yaw of the IMU chip relative to base_link by comparing the horizontal accelerometer vector to the wheel-odometry body-frame acceleration during the forward and backward ramps. Only samples with |wz| < 0.05 rad/s and |a_body| > 0.1 m/s² are used.
robotComponentEditor.calibRunningMessage: Calibration running — robot is driving itself
robotComponentEditor.calibRunningDescription: Forward leg, pause, backward leg. Stand clear. The motion will stop automatically.
robotComponentEditor.calibFailedHint: Hint: drive faster or longer so the accelerometer sees a clear forward and backward impulse along the body X axis.

dashboardHeroCard.crossedBoundaryHint: Lift the mower back inside the boundary, then resume.
dashboardHeroCard.emergencyStopHint: Check the mower is clear of obstacles, then release.
dashboardHeroCard.onDockPulling: On the dock, pulling {{amps}}A. I'll take over again once we hit 100%.
dashboardHeroCard.resumeWhenClears: I'll resume automatically when it clears.
dashboardHeroCard.driveAlongBoundary: Drive along the boundary, then finish recording
dashboardHeroCard.idleOffDockHint: Tap Home to send the robot back to the dock, or Start to mow from here.
dashboardHeroCard.idleDockedHint: Tap Start mowing to begin, or wait for the next scheduled run.
dashboardHeroCard.idleOffDockHintShort: Tap Home to dock, or Start to mow from here.
dashboardHeroCard.idleDockedHintShort: Tap Start to begin, or wait for the next scheduled run.

notificationBell.criticalEventsHint: Critical events (emergency, rain, completion) will land here.

logsPage.noLinesMatch: No lines match the active filters.

imuYawCalibration.timedOut: Timed out after 155 s. The service may still be running — check the robot and retry.

notificationCenter.emergencyBody: Firmware latched the e-stop. Check the robot, then tap Reset to clear.
notificationCenter.rainBody: Mowing paused. The robot will resume automatically once the rain clears.
notificationCenter.mowingCompleteBody: All scheduled areas have been mowed.
notificationCenter.boundaryBody: The robot stopped after leaving the area. Lift it back in, then resume.

hardware.label: Hardware
hardware.description: Robot model, wheels, chassis, and blade dimensions

drive_motor.label: Drive Motor
drive_motor.description: Firmware wheel-velocity PID gains and feedforward (applied live)

ntrip.label: NTRIP Corrections
ntrip.description: RTK correction network and base station — set this before GPS

positioning.label: GPS & Positioning
positioning.description: Universal GNSS receiver and map datum

sensors.label: Sensors
sensors.description: LiDAR, IMU, and GPS antenna placement on the robot

localization.label: Localization
localization.description: Map-frame fusion strategy and optional LiDAR factors

mowing.label: Mowing
mowing.description: Speed, swath/headland, angle, and outline settings

battery.label: Battery
battery.description: Voltage and percentage thresholds for charging

safety.label: Safety
safety.description: Emergency stops, temperature limits, obstacle avoidance

advanced.label: Advanced
advanced.description: Raw parameters and custom key-value pairs

toasts.savedGpsRestartDescription: Restarting GPS to apply GNSS changes. Restart ROS2 to apply other saved changes.
toasts.dockRefreshFailed: Settings saved, but map_server runtime refresh failed
toasts.dockRefreshFailedDescription: Restart ROS2 (or call /map_server_node/set_docking_point manually) to pick up the new dock pose.
toasts.drivePidUpdateFailed: Settings saved, but live drive-PID update failed
toasts.drivePidUpdateFailedDescription: Restart ROS2 to apply the new drive-motor PID gains.

ntripProviders.centipedeNote: Free community RTK network. Public login — just pick the nearest base.
ntripProviders.rtk2goNote: Free community casters worldwide. Use your email as the username.
ntripProviders.saposNote: Paid national service — enter your state's caster host and credentials.
ntripProviders.customNote: Enter any NTRIP caster host, port and (optional) login.
